_meta.criterion: AUTHOR_BRIEF criterion 19 (Rev 16): is the accent-stripped or hyper-accented twin a PLAUSIBLE ALTERNATIVE ANSWER to this item's own prompt?
_meta.schema_note: Extends the dispatch schema with: topic, axis, klass, flag_on_disk, action. add_guards is empty throughout: only one markpoint takes a new flag and its twin is already guarded.
_meta.data_caveat: grammar_questions_pronoun.json is TRUNCATED in the worktree (invalid JSON, mtime 10:37); its 9 axis-1 + 30 axis-2 markpoints were judged against git HEAD and must be re-verified after repair.
_meta.state_note: Corpus census taken 11:04 showed 16 flags; grammar_questions_verb_form.present_indicative.json was rewritten at 11:18 DURING this sweep, removing the 3 axis-1 flags (è, dà, può) and their dead guards. flag_on_disk re-read after that write: 13 flags. The removals match this sweep's independent verdicts. The dispatch's "present_indicative: 10 flagged" was always a miscount: it was 8 (3 axis-1 + 5 axis-2), now 5.

Metalinguistic MCQ: the answer string is 'sì (with accent)' and the distractor 'si (no accent)'. The two do not fold onto each other (the parentheticals differ), so the guard is live and no flag is needed. The item tests the accent by naming it rather than by typing it.

Answer is di' (apocope + apostrophe); the twin dì is not a typo but an attested older spelling of the same imperative, so it is a form a learner can genuinely reach for and believe. norm() folds the apostrophe, so without the flag dì folds onto di' and credits. Flag is doing real work.
entries.action: FLAG STANDS. Separate question for ImperativoAuthor: dì is a dated variant, not an error - Rev 20(i) allows a graded credit (0.5-0.8) with a steering note instead of a flat WRONG. Not my call.

Prompt subject is Marco (3sg); parlo is 1sg present, so a learner reaching for the present writes parla, not parlo. The twin is unreachable as an intended answer here - only as a dropped accent. FAILS criterion 19 as written. See thread: the -are class is where the criterion and the accent-as-morpheme principle diverge; Smith's call.
entries.action: REMOVE flag + guard parlo (pending Smith on the policy question); guard parlao stays

entries.why: Prompt subject is Nessuno (3sg); crede is the 3sg PRESENT - the right person, the wrong tense. This is a live present-tense attempt at the blank, so the accent alone separates two real answers. The only markpoint in the estate that passes criterion 19 on axis 1.
entries.action: FLAG STANDS (containment verified: crede is word-anchored, credette clears it). SEPARATE BUG: this markpoint's label reads '3sg crede / credette (dual, accent)' - it should be 'credé / credette'. The breadcrumb currently shows the learner 'crede', which this flag makes a hard WRONG. Route to PassatoRemotoAuthor.

entries.why: Prompt subject is Il bambino (3sg); dormi is 2sg present, so the present-tense reach for this subject is dorme, not dormi. Twin unreachable as an intended answer. FAILS criterion 19 as written. Same policy question as parlò.
entries.action: REMOVE flag + guard dormi (pending Smith); guard dormiva stays

entries.why: fùi is not a word; the learner formed the remoto correctly and added a spurious accent. That is orthography.accent.italian.added (shipped r8, manifest-registered), not a suppletive-formation miss. Flagging routes a spelling slip to a bucket the learner has just demonstrated mastery of.
entries.action: REMOVE flag + guard fùi; guard essei stays

entries.why: fù is not a word - the commonest hypercorrection in written Italian, and exactly what orthography.accent.italian.added exists to count. Same reasoning as fùi.
entries.action: REMOVE flag + guard fù; guard era stays (genuine wrong-tense catch)

entries.why: fù is not a word. Same as prem_supp_essere_3sg_02.
entries.action: REMOVE flag + guard fù; guard era stays
